customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, transactional posture. Customer is price-shopping — explicitly stated he does not know if he wants solar and is gathering cost information. No scam anxiety, no hostility. Cooperative but not emotionally invested.
unresolved_concerns: Customer's stated goal is to find out what solar costs — he has not expressed interest in savings, bill pain, or any emotional driver. The closer should expect a comparison-shopping mindset.
No co-decision-maker identified. Unknown whether a spouse or other party is on the title or involved in financial decisions.
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will be available tomorrow at noon.
The appointment will cover the design, price, and savings.
Implied phone appointment — but 'drop by' language was used; closer should confirm phone format in any pre-appointment outreach.
closer_should_know: Andrew booked to get a price, not because he is sold on solar. His frame is transactional and was never shifted. The closer should open by anchoring the winter usage data (January 2,254 kWh, February 3,498 kWh against a stated $300/month average) as the emotional entry point — that data exists and is credible, but the setter never used it. No co-decision-maker was identified; the closer should surface this early.
